root_text.an5.124:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato gilānupaṭṭhāko nālaṁ gilānaṁ upaṭṭhātuṁ.
root_text.an5.124:1.4: sappāyāsappāyaṁ na jānāti, asappāyaṁ upanāmeti, sappāyaṁ apanāmeti;
root_text.an5.124:1.6: jegucchī hoti uccāraṁ vā passāvaṁ vā vantaṁ vā kheḷaṁ vā nīharituṁ;
root_text.an5.124:1.7: nappaṭibalo hoti gilānaṁ kālena kālaṁ dhammiyā kathāya sandassetuṁ samādapetuṁ samuttejetuṁ sampahaṁsetuṁ.
root_text.an5.124:1.8: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato gilānupaṭṭhāko nālaṁ gilānaṁ upaṭṭhātuṁ.
root_text.an5.124:2.1: Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato gilānupaṭṭhāko alaṁ gilānaṁ upaṭṭhātuṁ.
root_text.an5.124:2.4: sappāyāsappāyaṁ jānāti, asappāyaṁ apanāmeti, sappāyaṁ upanāmeti;
root_text.an5.124:2.6: ajegucchī hoti uccāraṁ vā passāvaṁ vā vantaṁ vā kheḷaṁ vā nīharituṁ;
root_text.an5.124:2.7: paṭibalo hoti gilānaṁ kālena kālaṁ dhammiyā kathāya sandassetuṁ samādapetuṁ samuttejetuṁ sampahaṁsetuṁ.
root_text.an5.124:2.8: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato gilānupaṭṭhāko alaṁ gilānaṁ upaṭṭhātun”ti.

translation_text.an5.124:1.1: “Mendicants, a carer with five qualities is not competent to care for a patient.
translation_text.an5.124:1.4: They don’t know what is suitable and unsuitable, so they supply what is unsuitable and remove what is suitable.
translation_text.an5.124:1.5: They care for the sick for the sake of material benefits, not out of love.
translation_text.an5.124:1.6: They’re disgusted to remove feces, urine, vomit, or spit.
translation_text.an5.124:1.7: They’re unable to educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire the patient with a Dhamma talk from time to time.
translation_text.an5.124:1.8: A carer with these five qualities is not competent to care for a patient.
translation_text.an5.124:2.1: A carer with five qualities is competent to care for a patient.
translation_text.an5.124:2.4: They know what is suitable and unsuitable, so they remove what is unsuitable and supply what is suitable.
translation_text.an5.124:2.5: They care for the sick out of love, not for the sake of material benefits.
translation_text.an5.124:2.6: They’re not disgusted to remove feces, urine, vomit, or spit.
translation_text.an5.124:2.7: They’re able to educate, encourage, fire up, and inspire the patient with a Dhamma talk from time to time.
translation_text.an5.124:2.8: A carer with these five qualities is competent to care for a patient.”

variant_text.an5.124:1.7: nappaṭibalo → na paṭibalo (bj, sya-all, pts1ed) | samādapetuṁ → samādāpetuṁ (?)
